name: Maresin 1 improved aged mouse fracture healing
summary: MaR1 treatment after tibial fracture in aged mice reduced pro-inflammatory macrophage signals and improved late-stage bone repair mechanics.
statement: In 24-month-old mice after tibial fracture, MaR1 treatment reduced early pro-inflammatory macrophages and circulating inflammatory biomarkers, increased bone deposition, and improved structural stiffness and force to fracture during later repair.
population_or_model: Aged mice after tibial fracture surgery, with aged macrophage and stromal-cell culture assays
time_horizon: Treatment beginning 3 days after injury, with repair assessed through 28 days
quantitative_note: The finding supports aged-animal tissue repair through inflammation resolution, not human fracture-healing efficacy.
caveats: The model is an induced tibial fracture rather than broad age-related tissue repair.
The translational dose, route, and safety profile are unresolved.
